title: mod_perl Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and configurations for mod_perl usage.

commands.description: Load the mod_perl module into Apache.
commands.scenario: Use when setting up mod_perl with Apache to enable Perl scripting.

commands.description: Enable warnings and taint mode in Perl scripts.
commands.scenario: Use to enforce safer coding practices in your Perl scripts.

commands.description: Execute a Perl script at server startup.
commands.scenario: Use to initialize Perl modules or set environment variables on server start.

commands.description: Load a Perl module at server startup.
commands.scenario: Use to preload Perl modules for faster script execution.

commands.description: Configure a Perl handler for a specific location.
commands.scenario: Use to handle requests with a custom Perl script at a specific URL path.

commands.description: Enable parsing of HTTP headers by mod_perl.
commands.scenario: Use when you need mod_perl to handle HTTP headers in your response.

commands.description: Run Perl scripts under mod_perl's Registry mode.
commands.scenario: Use to execute Perl scripts with mod_perl's performance benefits while maintaining CGI compatibility.

commands.description: Set environment variables for Perl scripts.
commands.scenario: Use to configure database connection strings or other environment-specific settings.

commands.description: Limit memory usage of Perl processes.
commands.scenario: Use to prevent runaway processes from consuming too much memory.

commands.description: Run a Perl handler after reading the request but before processing it.
commands.scenario: Use to implement custom authentication or logging logic.

commands.description: Run a Perl handler when a child process is initialized.
commands.scenario: Use to perform initialization tasks specific to each child process.

commands.description: Modify the response body before sending it to the client.
commands.scenario: Use to compress or transform output data on-the-fly.
